title: Lumen GM Hub — Project Knowledge Base
content:
# brain/lumen.md — Project Knowledge Base
> Last updated: 2026-03-11

## What Is Lumen?

- Lumen GM Hub is an AI-powered companion app for PF2e tabletop RPG sessions run in Foundry VTT v13 [EXPLICIT]
- It captures session audio, transcribes in real time with speaker attribution, generates GM-facing suggestions, maintains campaign memory, detects continuity conflicts, and writes session notes to Foundry journals [EXPLICIT]
- PF2e only for v1 — system-agnostic is a non-goal [EXPLICIT]
- Windows 10/11 at launch; macOS later via Electron [EXPLICIT]

## Current Status

- **Phase:** PoC Complete — Go verdict reached (2026-03-11)
- **Priority:** Secondary to Sylvara (which is #1) [EXPLICIT]
- **Codebase:** `C:\Dev\Lumen` — PoC validated, ready for v0.1 product development
- **Spec version:** v3 (March 10, 2026)

## Key Facts

### Audio Architecture
- VoIP-first: built-in WebRTC voice client, NOT Discord loopback [EXPLICIT]
- Each player's audio arrives as an independent stream — speaker attribution is deterministic [EXPLICIT]
- pyannote diarization was evaluated and eliminated — per-stream capture makes it unnecessary [EXPLICIT]
- Virtual audio device (VB-Cable) was eliminated — VoIP replaces it entirely [EXPLICIT]
- Voice enrollment is not required for attribution (optional future feature) [EXPLICIT]
- Players join via browser link — no software install required [EXPLICIT]

### Transcription
- Using `faster-whisper` (CTranslate2 backend), not vanilla OpenAI Whisper [EXPLICIT]
- Silero VAD for speech boundary detection, runs per-stream [EXPLICIT]
- `large-v3` is the recommended default for all tiers including 8GB cards (~5.9 GB peak VRAM) [VALIDATED]
- `medium` is fallback only for heavily constrained 8GB systems [VALIDATED]
- Single shared Whisper instance with segment queue — VRAM does not scale with concurrent streams (CTranslate2 shares weights) [VALIDATED]
- VAD tuning: threshold 0.65, min speech 300ms, RMS energy gate 0.01 — eliminates hallucinations [VALIDATED]

### Hardware
- Cole's development/test hardware: RTX 3070 (8GB VRAM) [EXPLICIT]
- Minimum spec (local tier): 6 GB VRAM [EXPLICIT]
- Recommended spec (local tier): 8 GB VRAM [EXPLICIT]
- Optimal spec (local tier): 12+ GB VRAM [EXPLICIT]
- CUDA is required for faster-whisper [EXPLICIT]

### Business Model
- Two-tier subscription: local (free or $3–5/mo) and cloud premium ($12–20/mo) [EXPLICIT]
- Local tier: audio processed on GM's machine, strong privacy story [EXPLICIT]
- Cloud tier: audio processed on Lumen servers, no GPU required for GM [EXPLICIT]
- BYO API keys for suggestion engine (OpenAI / Anthropic) [EXPLICIT]
- Cloud tier is NOT a v1 feature — ship local first, add cloud later [EXPLICIT]

### Foundry Integration
- Foundry v13 module connects via WebSocket to Hub on localhost [EXPLICIT]
- Module is free; premium features require subscription [EXPLICIT]
- Write permissions are granular toggles — no auto-writes ever [EXPLICIT]
- Campaign Ledger served as read-only snapshot to players [EXPLICIT]

## Resolved Questions (PoC)

- [x] **Whisper model size on 3070:** `large-v3` fits comfortably — 5,851 MB peak, 6,349 MB avg under sustained 3-stream load alongside Foundry [Phase 1+2+3]
- [x] **Single vs. per-stream Whisper:** Single shared instance — CTranslate2 shares model weights, VRAM doesn't scale with streams [Phase 1]
- [x] **Pipeline stability:** 60 min, 16,548 segments, 0 errors, no VRAM leak, +1.5% latency drift [Phase 3]
- [x] **RNNoise needed?** No — WebRTC audio quality sufficient for accurate transcription without preprocessing [Phase 2]

## Open Questions (Post-PoC)

- [ ] Does Opus compression at 64kbps degrade Whisper accuracy meaningfully? (Not formally tested)
- [ ] What % of real-world connections need TURN relay? (Localhost-only validated)
- [ ] What's the actual cloud GPU cost per session-hour? (Post-PoC benchmark)
- [ ] LiveKit vs. alternatives for cloud tier media server? (Post-PoC decision)

## Planned Expansions

### Starfinder 2e Support (Target: v1.1 or v1.2)
- SF2e runs on the same core rules engine as PF2e — three-action economy, conditions, proficiency, skill checks are all shared [EXPLICIT]
- Mechanical delta is mostly domain content: tech weapons, vehicles, species (vs ancestries), starship combat, different skill applications [INFERRED]
- Suggestion engine and conflict detection are system-agnostic at the logic layer — they operate on transcript + memory + rules data regardless of whether the content is fantasy or sci-fi [INFERRED]
- Rules data will be sourced from Foundry's SF2e system module compendiums, same architecture as PF2e — no external APIs, no scraping [EXPLICIT]
- Lumen Foundry connector handles both system IDs; rest of the pipeline is shared [INFERRED]
- **Not a v1 feature.** PF2e ships first. SF2e added post-launch to avoid doubling QA surface for initial release [EXPLICIT]
- **Open question:** How mature is the Foundry SF2e system module? Does it have comparable compendium coverage to the PF2e module? Evaluate before committing to a release target [EXPLICIT]
- Third-party PF2e APIs (e.g. SargntSprinkles/Pathfinder-2e-API) were evaluated and rejected — too fragile, incomplete coverage, scraper-based [EXPLICIT]

## People & Stakeholders

- **Cole** — sole developer, product owner [EXPLICIT]
- No external stakeholders for Lumen (unlike Sylvara which has John and Eric at Iron Tree) [INFERRED]
- Target users: PF2e GMs running Foundry VTT [EXPLICIT]
- PF2e Foundry community is the initial market [EXPLICIT]

## Timeline

- PoC: 5–7 weeks part-time alongside Sylvara [EXPLICIT]
- Phase 1 (Whisper validation): 1 week [EXPLICIT]
- Phase 2 (VoIP + per-stream transcription): 2–3 weeks [EXPLICIT]
- Phase 3 (integrated pipeline + session test): 1–2 weeks [EXPLICIT]
- Phase 4 (verdict): 1–2 days [EXPLICIT]

## Related Files
- `projects/lumen/plan.md` — operational plan and PoC phases
- `projects/lumen/AGENTS.md` — Codex guidelines
- `projects/lumen/spec_v3.pdf` — full product specification
- `brain/lumen-architecture.md` — technical decisions and ADRs
- `brain/lumen-business.md` — business model and pricing
